content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: I purchased a resale ticket and my event status changed. What are my options?

  - url: https://help.ticketmaster.com/hc/en-us/articles/9715556904465-I-purchased-a-resale-ticket-and-my-event-status-changed-What-are-my-options

  - source: Ticketmaster Help

  - domain: help.ticketmaster.com


  ## Content


  # I purchased a resale ticket and my event status changed. What are my options?


  The next step depends on the purchase record behind the ticket that is currently
  displayed.


  For a verified resale ticket bought through Ticketmaster, event changes such as
  postponement, rescheduling, cancellation, or a venue move are handled through the
  Ticketmaster account associated with that purchase. Ticketmaster sends an email
  or account notification describing the options available for the affected event.
  Customers should sign in, open the event in their account, and review the current
  event-specific options.


  A ticket that appears in a Ticketmaster account is not always linked to a Ticketmaster-owned
  purchase order. Tickets can also reach a Ticketmaster account after being obtained
  elsewhere. The fact that the ticket is currently displayed in Ticketmaster therefore
  does not, by itself, establish who owns the underlying purchase transaction.


  For responsibility resolution, the current platform record should first be checked
  to determine whether it is linked to a direct Ticketmaster purchase order or whether
  additional origin tracing is required. Transfer-recipient cases are governed by
  separate Ticketmaster guidance that explains how refund responsibility is handled
  after transfer provenance is confirmed.


  The practical distinction at this stage is therefore between:


  1. a Ticketmaster-owned purchase record whose current event-change options can be
  handled through the associated Ticketmaster account; and

  2. a current Ticketmaster ticket record whose underlying purchase ownership has
  not yet been established and must be resolved before choosing a refund route.


  Ticketmaster also directs users to its Resale Purchase Policy and Ticket Transfer
  Recipient Policy for the detailed terms governing those cases.

  '
metadata: {}
title: I purchased a resale ticket and my event status changed. What are my options?
url: https://help.ticketmaster.com/hc/en-us/articles/9715556904465-I-purchased-a-resale-ticket-and-my-event-status-changed-What-are-my-options

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Third-Party Broker Tickets

  - url: https://broadwaysanjose.com/ticket-help/third-party-broker-tickets-guests-take-caution/

  - source: Broadway San Jose

  - domain: broadwaysanjose.com


  ## Content


  # Third-Party Broker Tickets


  ## Guests, Take Caution


  Broadway San Jose advises customers to buy tickets only through Ticketmaster, the
  San Jose Civic Box Office, or the box office at the San Jose Center for the Performing
  Arts.


  The venue organization states that it is not responsible for tickets purchased through
  unauthorized third parties. For broker purchases, Broadway San Jose cannot guarantee
  that the ticket is valid, guarantee admission, or replace a lost or stolen ticket.
  It also warns that duplicate, copied, altered, or counterfeit tickets will not be
  accepted.


  Broadway San Jose further notes that it cannot contact customers who purchased through
  an unauthorized broker with event-related updates such as time changes, cancellations,
  road closures, or similar notices. Tickets associated with unauthorized resale may
  also be revoked, and a revoked ticket does not qualify for admission or a refund
  from the venue organization.


  The page identifies the approved direct purchase channels for Broadway San Jose
  performances and separates those channels from unrelated third-party transactions.
  Questions about the venue''s ticket-buying warning can be directed to the San Jose
  Civic Box Office.

  '

# Bely Y Beto\
  \ Sue\xF1os Tour USA

The Bely Y Beto Sue\xF1os Tour USA performance scheduled\
  \ for Saturday, June 27, 2026 at 5:00 PM at the San Jose Center for the Performing\
  \ Arts in San Jose, California, was canceled by the Event Organizer.

The event\
  \ page marks the event as canceled and states that the standard Ticketmaster cancellation\
  \ process applies to eligible direct purchases. The notice identifies the event,\
  \ date, time, and venue and confirms that the cancellation came from the Event Organizer.
\
  
For questions about ticket-specific refund ownership, transferred tickets, or\
  \ purchases made through another resale source, customers must use the applicable\
  \ Ticketmaster Help and seller-specific policies rather than relying on the event-status\
  \ notice alone.
"

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Postponed, rescheduled, or canceled events

  - url: https://support.stubhub.com/articles/61000276300-postponed-rescheduled-or-canceled-events

  - source: StubHub Support

  - domain: support.stubhub.com


  ## Content


  # Postponed, rescheduled, or canceled events


  StubHub treats canceled events differently from postponed or rescheduled events.


  When an event is canceled and is not rescheduled, the buyer receives compensation
  for the affected StubHub purchase under StubHub''s current canceled-event process.
  The default option is a credit placed in the buyer''s StubHub Wallet worth 120%
  of the amount paid for the affected purchase.


  A buyer who prefers money back instead of the wallet credit can request a cash refund.
  The request is made from the StubHub Wallet by selecting the cash-refund option
  attached to the appropriate canceled-event credit. StubHub issues the cash refund
  back to the original payment method used for the purchase.


  For postponed or rescheduled events, the existing ticket normally remains valid
  for the new date, and the buyer may resell it if unable to attend. StubHub does
  not treat a rescheduled event the same way as a canceled event.


  The canceled-event procedure is tied to the buyer''s StubHub purchase and wallet
  record. A person seeking the cash-refund option should therefore use the purchase
  account that owns the affected StubHub transaction and select the corresponding
  canceled-event credit.

  '

content.content:
I verified that the refund is handled by **StubHub**, not Ticketmaster.

- The ticket visible in Ticketmaster is an accepted transfer.
- There is no direct Ticketmaster purchase order associated with it.
- The original purchase was through StubHub: **SH-ORD-58391274**.
- The event was confirmed canceled.
- No existing refund case was found.

I submitted a **cash refund to the original payment method** through StubHub.  
**Confirmation:** `SH-RFD-260707-1842`

final_answer:
I verified that the refund is handled by **StubHub**, not Ticketmaster.

- The ticket visible in Ticketmaster is an accepted transfer.
- There is no direct Ticketmaster purchase order associated with it.
- The original purchase was through StubHub: **SH-ORD-58391274**.
- The event was confirmed canceled.
- No existing refund case was found.

I submitted a **cash refund to the original payment method** through StubHub.  
**Confirmation:** `SH-RFD-260707-1842`
